steps.title: Identify the key data point
steps.content: The study found that participants recruited through social networks showed much higher retention than those recruited by strangers. This comparison is the key to understanding what the finding suggests.

steps.title: Determine what the comparison suggests
steps.content:
Choice A directly identifies the mechanism: social connection creates obligation to persist.

Choices B and C address recruitment likelihood and regional effectiveness (neither explained in the passage), while D contradicts the advantage by implying social networks include strangers.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
